shortAnswer: Blood urea nitrogen (BUN) is produced when protein is broken down and excreted by the kidneys. Elevated levels may indicate reduced kidney function or a high-protein diet. Discuss abnormal findings with your doctor.
metaDescription: BUN / blood urea nitrogen elevated? Normal ranges, causes, and what it means for kidney health: explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Is my BUN related to my kidney function?
Should I adjust my protein intake?
Do I need further kidney tests?
pageTitle: BUN value: Blood Urea Nitrogen explained | Medyra
